_provenance.stage: Stage 6 — Synthesis (retroactive rebuild from Codex Stage 3)

revision_log: REMOVED (Stage 3): MISLEADING — Headline 'RM4.8B' specific national cost anchor (unpublished model estimate; no primary source verifies a national cross-subsidy total).
REMOVED (Stage 3): UNVERIFIED — 'Average Bumiputera lot take-up rate is 54%' (cited figure could not be traced to a verifiable dataset).

CORRECTED (Stage 3): INCORRECT — '46% of discounted units unsold, released after 6-18 months' → 'June 2020 REHDA-cited survey reported 6,121 unsold quota units; 46% had been on the books over a year' (CIDB-hosted source).

REPHRASED (Stage 3): MISLEADING — 'No means-testing applies to the Bumiputera discount' → 'broad open-market discounts are described as not means-tested; affordable-housing schemes may apply income criteria separately'.

REPHRASED (Stage 3): MISLEADING — 'Malaysia's system benefits one group at another's direct expense, with no reciprocal obligation' → cross-system comparison framed with care (EIP applies to buyers and sellers; Malaysia's applies on buyer side only).
REPHRASED (Stage 3): MISLEADING — 'Private tax on one group for another' → 'developer-administered cross-subsidy borne by open-market buyers' (Stage 3 wording).

REPHRASED (Stage 3): UNVERIFIED — View 'creates resentment because costs are visible and unequal' → 'industry-administered cross-subsidies hide their costs and complicate reform' (instrument critique only).
REPHRASED: Reframe restructured to question the instrument, not the policy goal of Bumiputera housing access.

3R-CARE NOTE: 3R race-adjacent issue (FAS=34). Critique scoped to the instrument (cross-subsidy vs revenue-funded) and to factual transparency, not to the community or the policy goal. Every specific number now traces to a citable source or has been removed.
CLEARED legacyAudit (rebuild complete per CLAUDE.md Accuracy Standard).

PRESERVED (Stage 3 VERIFIED): 5-15% discount range across states; developers recover discount through open-market prices; cross-funded not government-funded; Singapore EIP applies to buyers and sellers.
